title: Bay of Islands June
meta_desc: Discover the best of Bay of Islands in June with kids: weather, events, crowd levels, and practical tips for a fun family holiday in NZ's winterless north
intro: June in the Bay of Islands is a great time to visit with kids, with mild winter weather, fewer crowds, and a range of exciting activities to enjoy. The region's subtropical climate means average highs of 17°C and average lows of 9°C, making it ideal for outdoor adventures. From dolphin watching cruises to exploring the Waitangi Treaty Grounds, there's something for everyone in the Bay of Islands

sections.heading: Weather and What's On
sections.body:
June is the start of winter in New Zealand, but the Bay of Islands is known for its mild climate, making it an ideal destination for families. Average temperatures range from 9°C to 17°C, with an average of 12 hours of sunlight per day. This month, the Bay of Islands Winter Festival takes place, featuring live music, food stalls, and activities for kids.

The festival is a great way to experience the local culture and community, and many of the events are free or low-cost, making it accessible to families on a budget. Some popular events include the Winter Festival Parade, the Bay of Islands Market, and the Waitangi Winter Festival Concert.

sections.heading: Best Activities for June
sections.body:
June is a great time to enjoy the Bay of Islands' outdoor activities, such as sailing, swimming, and hiking. The calm winter waters make it ideal for kayaking, paddleboarding, or taking a dolphin watching cruise. The Waitangi Treaty Grounds are also a must-visit, with its rich history and cultural significance.

For a unique experience, take a sailing trip to a private island, where you can enjoy a picnic lunch and snorkel in the crystal-clear waters. The Haruru Falls Walk is another highlight, with its stunning scenery and easy walking trails.
list: Hole in the Rock (Cape Brett cruise, $80-$100 per person)

sections.heading: Crowd Levels and Bookings
sections.body:
Crowd levels in the Bay of Islands are generally lower in June, making it a great time to visit if you prefer fewer tourists. However, some popular activities and attractions may still require booking in advance, especially during school holidays.

It's recommended to book your accommodation, dolphin watching cruises, and Waitangi Treaty Grounds visits at least 2-3 weeks in advance to avoid disappointment. You can book online or through a local tour operator.
list: Book dolphin watching cruises at least 2 weeks in advance
Book Waitangi Treaty Grounds visits at least 1 week in advance

sections.heading: Take the Ferry to Russell
sections.body:
One of the cheapest and most enjoyable family outings in June is the short passenger ferry from Paihia across to historic Russell — New Zealand's first permanent European settlement. The crossing takes about 15 minutes, costs only a few dollars per person (kids are heavily discounted), and runs frequently year-round. In winter the ferries are quiet, so you'll often have plenty of room.

Russell itself is a gentle, walkable village: wander the waterfront, visit the historic Christ Church (NZ's oldest), grab fish and chips by the wharf, and let the kids explore the small museum. It's a low-cost, low-key half-day that works perfectly on a cool June day. If you're driving, the vehicle ferry from Opua is the alternative, but the foot-passenger ferry from Paihia is far cheaper for families.
list: Paihia–Russell passenger ferry — ~15 mins, only a few dollars, kids discounted
Frequent year-round service; quiet and uncrowded in June
Russell waterfront, NZ's oldest church, and a small museum to explore
Cheaper than the Opua vehicle ferry for a family on foot

sections.heading: Whale & Dolphin Watching Season
sections.body: June marks the start of the winter marine season in Northland's waters. Dolphins are present year-round and the calm winter seas make sightings comfortable, while the cooler months bring increased chances of seeing migrating whales offshore. Operators run smaller winter groups, so trips feel more personal — and prices are often lower than summer. Choose an operator with a guaranteed-sighting policy (a free repeat trip if you don't see dolphins), bundle warm windproof layers, and book a day or two in case of weather.

sections.heading: Sample Activity Costs
sections.body: Here's a guide to what June activities cost for a family of four.

rows: Cheapest family outing in the bay
Smaller winter groups; check sighting guarantee
Often quieter and cheaper in winter
Look for family rates; under-5s usually free

sections.heading: Practical Tips for Families
sections.body:
When visiting the Bay of Islands with kids, it's essential to be prepared for the winter weather. Pack warm clothing, including jackets, hats, and gloves, as well as waterproof gear for outdoor activities.

Additionally, consider booking accommodation with a kitchen or self-catering facilities to save money on food and snacks. Many apartments and holiday homes in Paihia offer these facilities, starting from around $200 per night for a family of four.
list: Pack warm clothing and waterproof gear
Bring sunscreen and sunhats for outdoor activities
Check the weather forecast before heading out
